summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is a polygon?

Polygons A Polygon is a closed figure formed by three or more Line segments (sides) that meet at their endpoints (vertices). The other options — 'Any curved shape', 'A figure with exactly two sides', and 'An open figure made of rays' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is a regular polygon?

Regular Polygon A regular Polygon is both equilateral (all sides congruent) and equiangular (all angles congruent). The other options — 'Any polygon with all sides equal', 'A polygon with all right angles', and 'A polygon with at least 6 sides' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is the perimeter of a polygon?

Perimeter The perimeter of a Polygon is the sum of the lengths of its sides. Polygons A Polygon is a closed figure formed by three or more Line segments (sides) that meet at their endpoints (vertices). The other options — 'The area enclosed by the polygon', 'The longest diagonal', and 'The number of sides' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.
